title: 999 and A&E emergency red flags
content: Call 999 or go to A&E for life-threatening emergencies: signs of a heart attack (central chest pain or pressure, pain spreading to arm or jaw, sweating); signs of a stroke (FAST: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call 999); severe difficulty breathing; anaphylaxis; heavy uncontrollable bleeding; seizures; unconsciousness or unresponsiveness; a sudden severe headache. These must never be booked as routine GP appointments and always escalate.
